text:
Securitisation exposures are addressed in the securitisation standard.1 Credit equivalent amounts
of OTC derivatives, exchange traded derivatives and long-settlement transactions that expose a bank to
counterparty credit risk2 are to be calculated under the counterparty credit risk standards.3 Equity
investments in funds and exposures to central counterparties must be treated according to their own
specific frameworks.4
1 The securitisation standard is available at www.bis.org/bcbs/publ/d374.pdf
obligation_gt: calculate credit equivalent amounts under counterparty credit risk standards for derivatives and long-settlement transactions
condition_gt: for OTC derivatives, exchange traded derivatives, and long-settlement transactions that expose to counterparty credit risk

text:
Consistent with the Committee’s guidance on the assessment of credit risk5 and paragraphs 733
to 735 of the Basel II framework (June 2006), banks must perform due diligence to ensure that they have
an adequate understanding, at origination and thereafter on a regular basis (at least annually), of the risk
profile and characteristics of their counterparties.
obligation_gt: must perform due diligence to ensure that they have an adequate understanding of the risk profile and characteristics of their counterparties
condition_gt: at origination and thereafter on a regular basis

text:
Banks must take reasonable and adequate steps to assess the
operating and financial performance levels and trends through internal credit analysis and/or other
analytics outsourced to a third party, as appropriate for each counterparty.
obligation_gt: must take reasonable and adequate steps to assess the operating and financial performance levels and trends through internal credit analysis and/or other analytics outsourced to a third party
condition_gt: as appropriate for each counterparty

text:
Banks must be able to access
information about their counterparties on a regular basis to complete due diligence analyses.
obligation_gt: must be able to access information about their counterparties to complete due diligence analyses
condition_gt: on a regular basis

text:
Banks must be able to demonstrate to
their supervisors that their due diligence analyses are appropriate.
obligation_gt: must be able to demonstrate to their supervisors that their due diligence analyses are appropriate

text:
As part of their supervisory review,
supervisors should ensure that banks have appropriately performed their due diligence analyses, and
should take supervisory measures where these have not been done.
obligation_gt: ensure that banks have appropriately performed their due diligence analyses, and take supervisory measures where these have not been done
condition_gt: as part of their supervisory review

text:
To qualify, an ECA must publish its risk
scores and subscribe to the OECD-agreed methodology.
obligation_gt: must publish its risk scores and subscribe to the OECD-agreed methodology
condition_gt: To qualify

text:
The eligibility criteria for MDBs risk-weighted at 0% are:
(i) very high-quality long-term issuer ratings, ie a majority of an MDB’s external ratings must be
AAA;12
10 The following examples outline how PSEs might be categorised when focusing on one specific feature, namely revenue-raising
powers.
obligation_gt: must have very high-quality long-term issuer ratings with a majority of external ratings being AAA
condition_gt: for eligibility for 0% risk-weighting
threshold_gt: majority of external ratings must be AAA

text:
12 MDBs that request to be added to the list of MDBs eligible for a 0% risk weight must comply with the AAA rating criterion at
the time of the application.
subject_gt: MDBs that request to be added to the list of MDBs eligible for a 0% risk weight
obligation_gt: must comply with the AAA rating criterion
condition_gt: request to be added to the list of MDBs eligible for a 0% risk weight

text:
Such ratings must not incorporate assumptions of implicit
government support, unless the rating refers to a public bank owned by its government.16 Banks
incorporated in jurisdictions that allow the use of external ratings for regulatory purposes must only apply
SCRA for their unrated bank exposures, in accordance with paragraph 21.
subject_gt: Banks incorporated in jurisdictions that allow the use of external ratings for regulatory purposes
obligation_gt: must only apply SCRA for their unrated bank exposures, in accordance with paragraph 21
condition_gt: incorporated in jurisdictions that allow the use of external ratings for regulatory purposes

text:
Banks must perform due diligence to ensure that the external ratings appropriately and
conservatively reflect the creditworthiness of the bank counterparties.
obligation_gt: must perform due diligence to ensure that the external ratings appropriately and conservatively reflect the creditworthiness of the bank counterparties

text:
A+ to A– etc), the bank must assign a risk weight at least one bucket higher than the “base” risk weight
determined by the external rating.

condition_gt: ratings in the A+ to A– range

text:
Due diligence analysis must never result in the application of a lower
risk weight than that determined by the external rating.
obligation_gt: Due diligence analysis must never result in the application of a lower risk weight than that determined by the external rating
condition_gt: Due diligence analysis
threshold_gt: risk weight not lower than external rating determination

text:
A counterparty bank classified into Grade A must meet or exceed the published minimum
regulatory requirements and buffers established by its national supervisor as implemented in the
jurisdiction where it is incorporated, except for bank-specific minimum regulatory requirements or buffers
that may be imposed through supervisory actions (eg via Pillar 2) and not made public.
subject_gt: A counterparty bank classified into Grade A
obligation_gt: must meet or exceed the published minimum regulatory requirements and buffers established by its national supervisor as implemented in the jurisdiction where it is incorporated
condition_gt: classified into Grade A
threshold_gt: published minimum regulatory requirements and buffers
exception_gt: except for bank-specific minimum regulatory requirements or buffers that may be imposed through supervisory actions (eg via Pillar 2) and not made public

text:
If such minimum
regulatory requirements and buffers (other than bank-specific minimum requirements or buffers) are not
publicly disclosed or otherwise made available by the counterparty bank then the counterparty bank must
be assessed as Grade B or lower.
obligation_gt: must be assessed as Grade B or lower
condition_gt: if minimum regulatory requirements and buffers (other than bank-specific minimum requirements or buffers) are not publicly disclosed or otherwise made available
exception_gt: bank-specific minimum requirements or buffers

text:
If as part of its due diligence, a bank assesses that a counterparty bank does not meet the
definition of Grade A in paragraphs 22 and 23, exposures to the counterparty bank must be classified as
Grade B or Grade C.
obligation_gt: exposures to the counterparty bank must be classified as Grade B or Grade C
condition_gt: if as part of its due diligence, a bank assesses that a counterparty bank does not meet the definition of Grade A in paragraphs 22 and 23

text:
A counterparty bank classified into Grade B must meet or exceed the published minimum
regulatory requirements (excluding buffers) established by its national supervisor as implemented in the
18 Under the Standardised Credit Risk Assessment Approach, exposures to banks without an external credit rating may receive a
risk weight of 30%, provided that the counterparty bank has a CET1 ratio which meets or exceeds 14% and a Tier 1 leverage
ratio which meets or exceeds 5%.
subject_gt: exposures to banks without an external credit rating
obligation_gt: may receive a risk weight of 30%
condition_gt: provided that the counterparty bank has a CET1 ratio which meets or exceeds 14% and a Tier 1 leverage ratio which meets or exceeds 5%
threshold_gt: CET1 ratio which meets or exceeds 14% and a Tier 1 leverage ratio which meets or exceeds 5%

text: The counterparty bank must also satisfy all the requirements for Grade A classification.
obligation_gt: satisfy all the requirements for Grade A classification

text:
If such minimum regulatory
requirements are not publicly disclosed or otherwise made available by the counterparty bank then the
counterparty bank must be assessed as Grade C.
obligation_gt: must be assessed as Grade C
condition_gt: If such minimum regulatory requirements are not publicly disclosed or otherwise made available by the counterparty bank

text:
At a minimum, if any of the following triggers is breached, a bank must classify the exposure into
Grade C:
• The counterparty bank does not meet the criteria for being classifed as Grade B with respect to
its published minimum regulatory requirements, as set out in paragraphs 25 and 26;
obligation_gt: must classify the exposure into Grade C
condition_gt: if any of the following triggers is breached: The counterparty bank does not meet the criteria for being classified as Grade B with respect to its published minimum regulatory requirements, as set out in paragraphs 25 and 26

text:
In that case, the exposure to such counterparty bank must be classified into
Grade C.
subject_gt: the exposure to such counterparty bank
obligation_gt: must be classified into Grade C
condition_gt: In that case, the exposure to such counterparty bank

text:
Proceeds deriving from the issue of these
bonds must be invested in conformity with the law in assets which, during the whole period of the validity
of the bonds, are capable of covering claims attached to the bonds and which, in the event of the failure
of the issuer, would be used on a priority basis for the reimbursement of the principal and payment of the
accrued interest.
obligation_gt: must invest proceeds from bond issuance in conformity with law in assets capable of covering claims and used on priority basis for reimbursement in case of failure
condition_gt: proceeds deriving from the issue of bonds
deadline_gt: during the whole period of the validity of the bonds

text:
In order to be eligible for the risk weights set out in paragraph 35, the underlying assets (the
cover pool) of covered bonds as defined in paragraph 32 shall meet the requirements set out in paragraph
34 and shall include any of the following:
• claims on, or guaranteed by, sovereigns, their central banks, public sector entities or multilateral
development banks;

obligation_gt: underlying assets (cover pool) shall meet requirements and include claims on or guaranteed by sovereigns, central banks, public sector entities or multilateral development banks

The conditions set out in this paragraph must be satisfied at the inception of the covered bond
and throughout its remaining maturity.

condition_gt: at the inception of the covered bond and throughout its remaining maturity
deadline_gt: at the inception of the covered bond and throughout its remaining maturity

text:
Covered bonds that meet the criteria set out in the paragraphs 33 and 34 shall be risk-weighted
based on the issue-specific rating or the issuer’s risk weight according to the rules outlined in paragraphs
98 to 116.
subject_gt: Covered bonds that meet the criteria set out in the paragraphs 33 and 34
obligation_gt: shall be risk-weighted based on the issue-specific rating or the issuer's risk weight according to the rules outlined in paragraphs 98 to 116

conservatively reflect the creditworthiness of the covered bond and the issuing bank.

A+ to A– etc), the bank must assign a risk weight at least one bucket higher than the

text:

A+ to A– etc), the bank must assign a risk weight at least

text:
Debt obligations and other securities, partnerships, derivatives or other vehicles structured with
the intent of conveying the economic substance of equity ownership are considered an equity holding.26
This includes liabilities from which the return is linked to that of equities.27 Conversely, equity investments
that are structured with the intent of conveying the economic substance of debt holdings or securitisation
exposures would not be considered an equity holding.28
25 For certain obligatio
obligation_gt: Must classify debt obligations, securities, partnerships, derivatives or other vehicles structured with the intent of conveying economic substance of equity ownership as equity holdings, including liabilities with returns linked to equities; must not classify equity investments structured with intent of conveying economic substance of debt holdings or securitisation exposures as equity holdings
condition_gt: When instruments are structured with the intent of conveying the economic substance of equity ownership OR when liabilities have returns linked to equities OR when equity investments are structured with intent of conveying economic substance of debt holdings or securitisation exposures
exception_gt: Equity investments that are structured with the intent of conveying the economic substance of debt holdings or securitisation exposures would not be considered an equity holding

text:

text:

text:

by the property, including relevant metrics (such as an occupancy rate of the property).

text:
Banks must include all balance sheet assets in their leverage ratio exposure measure, including
on-balance sheet derivatives collateral and collateral for SFTs, with the exception of on-balance sheet
derivative and SFT assets that are covered in paragraphs 32 to 56 below.11
29.
obligation_gt: must include all balance sheet assets in their leverage ratio exposure measure, including on-balance sheet derivatives collateral and collateral for SFTs
exception_gt: on-balance sheet derivative and SFT assets that are covered in paragraphs 32 to 56 below

text:

trade date accounting must reverse out any offsetting between cash receivables for unsettled sales and
cash payables for unsettled purchases of financial assets that may be recognised under the applicable
accounting framework, but may offset between those cash receivables and cash payables (regardless of
whether such offsetting is recognised under the applicable accounting framework) if the following
conditions are met:
• the fi
obligation_gt: must reverse out any offsetting between cash receivables for unsettled sales and cash payables for unsettled purchases of financial assets that may be recognised under the applicable accounting framework, but may offset between those cash receivables and cash payables if specified conditions are met

exception_gt: may offset between those cash receivables and cash payables if the financial assets bought and sold that are associated with cash payables and receivables are fair valued through income and included in the bank's regulatory trading book as specified by paragraphs 8 to 20 of the market risk framework

text:

text:
Treatment of clearing services: where a bank acting as clearing member (CM)19 offers clearing
services to clients, the CM’s trade exposures to the central counterparty (CCP) that arise when the CM is
obligated to reimburse the client for any losses suffered due to changes in the value of its transactions in
the event that the CCP defaults must be captured by applying the same treatment that applies to any
other type of derivative transaction.
obligation_gt: must capture trade exposures to the central counterparty (CCP) by applying the same treatment that applies to any other type of derivative transaction
condition_gt: where CM offers clearing services to clients and CM is obligated to reimburse the client for any losses suffered due to changes in the value of its transactions in the event that the CCP defaults

text:
In addition, where a bank provides clearing services as a “higher level client” within a multi-level
client structure,20 the bank need not recognise in its leverage ratio exposure measure the resulting trade
exposures to the CM or to an entity that serves as a higher level client to the bank in the leverage ratio
exposure measure if it meets all of the following conditions:
• The offsetting transactions are identified by the QCCP as higher level client transactions and
collateral to support them
obligation_gt: need not recognise in its leverage ratio exposure measure the resulting trade exposures to the CM or to an entity that serves as a higher level client to the bank in the leverage ratio exposure measure
condition_gt: where a bank provides clearing services as a "higher level client" within a multi-level client structure and meets all of the following conditions: The offsetting transactions are identified by the QCCP as higher level client transactions and collateral to support them
exception_gt: bank need not recognise trade exposures in leverage ratio exposure measure when conditions are met

condition_gt: Where a client enters directly into a derivative transaction with the CCP and the CM guarantees the performance of its client's derivative trade exposures to the CCP

text:
As such, where

reverse all sales-related accounting entries, and then calculate its exposure as if the SFT had been treated

its leverage ratio exposure measure.

text:
For the purposes of the leverage ratio exposure measure, the following will apply:
(a) Banks may net transactions subject to novation under which any obligation between a bank and
its counterparty to deliver a given currency on a given value date is automatically amalgamated
with all other obligations for the same currency and value date, legally substituting one single
amount for the previous gross obligations.
obligation_gt: may net transactions subject to novation under which any obligation between a bank and its counterparty to deliver a given currency on a given value date is automatically amalgamated with all other obligations for the same currency and value date, legally substituting one single amount for the previous gross obligations
condition_gt: For the purposes of the leverage ratio exposure measure

text:
(c) In both cases (a) and (b), a bank will need to satisfy its national supervisors that it has:
(i) a netting contract or agreement with the counterparty that creates a single legal obligation,
covering all included transactions, such that the bank would have either a claim to receive or
obligation to pay only the net sum of the positive and negative mark-to-market values of
included individual transactions in the event that a counterparty fails to perform due to any
of the following: default,
obligation_gt: satisfy its national supervisors that it has a netting contract or agreement with the counterparty that creates a single legal obligation, covering all included transactions, such that the bank would have either a claim to receive or obligation to pay only the net sum of the positive and negative mark-to-market values of included individual transactions
condition_gt: in the event that a counterparty fails to perform due to default

text:
The national supervisor, after consultation when necessary with other relevant supervisors,
must be satisfied that the netting is enforceable under the laws of each of the relevant
jurisdictions;36 and
(iii) procedures in place to ensure that the legal characteristics of netting arrangements are kept
under review in the light of possible changes in relevant law.
obligation_gt: must be satisfied that the netting is enforceable under the laws of each of the relevant jurisdictions
condition_gt: after consultation when necessary with other relevant supervisors

or bankruptcy of the counterparty;
